question: company goals by proposing a novel recommendation system for internal business processes, grounded in the COBIT 2019 framework. The system employs graph algorithms to map relationships, semantic similarity measure- ments to align enterprise goals with management objectives, and LSTM combined with TF-IDF methods to analyze and recommend specific activities. Experimental results show that this system optimizes IT management practices, enhances operational efficiency, and ensures regulatory compliance. Additionally, a user-friendly graphical representation of internal business pro- cesses using Neo4j enables stakeholders to visualize complex relationships within the COBIT framework, facilitating informed decision-making. This research advances IT governance practices by providing organizations with a valuable tool to automate and improve IT strategies, effectively achieving business objectives. Index Terms—COBIT 2019, graph algorithms, IT

The motivation stems from the challenges faced by auditors when identifying essential activities aligned with strategy of a company, often hindered by manual COBIT 2019 guideline navigation. To address this, a system was developed, where inputting the company strategy, triggers the display of recommended activities automatically. The methodology involved creating interconnected nodes from COBIT 2019 Element in Neo4J. This research uses a real strategy plan case study from 3 different companies as input, followed by a preprocessing step, then compares it to Enterprise Goals using TF-IDF and cosine similarity. question: the need for extra work. V. CONCLUSION V. CONCLUSION This paper propose a novel recommendation system for internal business processes based on the COBIT 2019 frame- work. By integrating graph algorithms and similarity methods, our approach provides tailored activity recommendations that align with an organization’s strategic goals. We mapped enter- prise goals to management objectives, identified relevant ac- tivities, and used graph queries to retrieve actionable insights. Utilizing Cypher Query Language (CQL) and Neo4j’s graph database, we created an interactive graphical representation of COBIT 2019-governed processes. This allows organizations to visualize relationships between COBIT elements, facilitating deeper analysis, real-time monitoring, and informed IT man- agement decisions. Our results demonstrated the system’s effectiveness in sug- gesting activities tailored to enterprise goals,

The motivation stems from the challenges faced by auditors when identifying essential activities aligned with strategy of a company, often hindered by manual COBIT 2019 guideline navigation. To address this, a system was developed, where inputting the company strategy, triggers the display of recommended activities automatically. The methodology involved creating interconnected nodes from COBIT 2019 Element in Neo4J. This research uses a real strategy plan case study from 3 different companies as input, followed by a preprocessing step, then compares it to Enterprise Goals using TF-IDF and cosine similarity. Goals with high similarity scores trigger a graph query to reveal connected

There are previous research on radar waveform for ISAC purposes , which discussed about cyclic prefix (CP) based OFDM waveform and how the CP is very much defining the maximum unambiguous range of the radar system. CC-OFDM MIMO radar waveform design which is discussed in this paper is not a CP based radar, the transmitters are transmitting multiple orthogonal symbols in series without any guard time

ABSTRACT Unlike the phased array (PA) radar that achieves array gain in proportion to the number of elements, the MIMO radar provides improvement to parameter estimation, target detection, and so on. Recently, the combination of the PA and MIMO concepts into the Phased MIMO (PMIMO) radar was proposed with overlapping non-coherent subarrays at the transmitter and an array with non-coherent elements at the receiver that proved to outperform the more conventional radars. In a previous patent , simultaneous multiple beams are generated using OFDM multiple antennas being fed at different points along a trans- mission line providing delay or phase differences between successive antennas, but the use of such a physical delay source limits its capability and ﬂexibility. On the other hand, OFDM signals with various delays can be produced with CC by ﬂexibly exploiting a programmable waveform gener- ator (WG). In the CC-OFDM MIMO radar, a novel technique can be used further to mitigate beam broadening for large scanning angles. In a way, the strategy of CC-OFDM MIMO radar can be viewed as a realization of hybrid between phased array and MIMO techniques , .

ABSTRACT Unlike the phased array (PA) radar that achieves array gain in proportion to the number of elements, the MIMO radar provides improvement to parameter estimation, target detection, and so on. Recently, the combination of the PA and MIMO concepts into the Phased MIMO (PMIMO) radar was proposed with overlapping non-coherent subarrays at the transmitter and an array with non-coherent elements at the receiver that proved to outperform the more conventional radars. question: together with transmit scheduling to avoid transmit receive coupling. The receive scheduling is also related to the time scheduling of the correlation between symbols, which is an important stage in the beam and range processing. The transmit and receive time schedule of CC-OFDM radar is described in Fig. 4. The receive scheduling is very important, especially in a multiple target scheme. The echo signals caused by two or more targets at different ranges (i.e., one target is near to the radar, and the other is far) could cause range ambiguity. ground_truth: In our case, quasi-continuous transmission is used through proper scheduling of OFDM transmission and echoes reception to prevent the coupling. • The receiver that continuously receives signal echoes coming from multiple targets must be able to distinguish which targets are very near or very far from the radar. This is the reason in this paper that orthogonal codes are given to successive CC-OFDM symbols. These unique codes are given and known by the receiver, so that the receiver could properly detect and distinguish targets nearby and far away from the radar. ground_truth: the transmitter design for long-range surveillance radar has been described to clarify the concept and demonstrate the potential advan- tages of the CC-OFDM MIMO radar. By using the proposed design, a range resolution of 52 meter and an azimuth resolu- tion of 1.7◦ can be achieved. For the multi-beam scheme, the beam isolation of −30 dB can be achieved for both between the co-channel beams and between the neighboring beams. The receiver design of CC-OFDM radar, including the beam, range and Doppler processing of the received signal will be discussed in a separate paper.

Within DSS01, organizations are required to identify specific service requirements, including performance and reliability criteria, and manage user expectations regarding IT services. Additionally, this domain also covers the measurement and monitoring of service performance, as well as the adjustment processes necessary to ensure that the services provided remain in line with established standards. ground_truth: Existing anomaly detection modelling techniques can be conducted either in a point, contextual, or collective setting. Point anomaly detection characterises each log entry, whether an event is an abnormality or a normal event. For instance, if a user types the wrong password when trying to gain access to an operating system prompt, the generated log message can be considered as an anomaly , , . As for contextual anomaly, the detection involves analysing some contextually related attributes to determine whether a log entry is an anomaly. In the drone context, a log message contains a description of occurring events which are triggered by various components such as sensors, peripherals, and firmware. Analysing the log message means analysing events from all aspects of the drone, including sensors, components and features . Typically, existing log-based anomaly detection stud- ies classify log events into two categories: normal and anomaly . question: classify log events into two categories: normal and anomaly . This is helpful in an online setting, where the number of detected abnormalities in a certain period of time is considerably small. However, in a digital forensic setting, where the detection is performed on all collected artefacts which might be large in size, the number of the abnormalities is likely to be large . Thus, analysing the detection result in a binary setting is impractical for the investigator. Moreover, out of all detected peculiarities, there might be several negligible ones with less severe impacts on the system or less likely to be related to an incident. Extending binary to multiclass anomaly detection can provide more detailed and contextual detection results
answer: As noted in the literature, analysing a large number of log entries resulting from binary anomaly detection is considered inefficient and labor-intensive, highlighting the need for a multiclass approach to improve detection efficiency (Silahi et al., 2025).

ground_truth: LLMs to detect anomalous log entries in a point-based approach . However, existing studies only detect whether an event or subsequent event is an anomaly. In a forensic setting, analysing a large number of log entries resulting from binary anomaly detection is considered inefficient and labour-intensive as the detection is performed on the acquired evidence at one time . Therefore, too many detected anomalies need to be detailed into a more fine- grained detection to help the investigator better prioritise the analysis objective. As a result, this study extends the binary anomaly detection to multi-class anomaly detection, as exemplified by another domain application such as plane flight data , image data , and internet of vehicle . question: comparison of the proposed framework, several baselines are constructed, including those that are proposed in published works. This paper proposes a transformer encoder-based log- based anomaly detection optimised using multitask label representation to help the model learn from the overlapped features shared by the samples from different severity levels. A domain-specific decoding procedure is also proposed to infer the prediction result. In-depth analyses are performed to evaluate the proposed framework thoroughly. The main contributions of this paper are summarised as follows: 1) Propose Drone Log Severity (DroLoVe), a severity- oriented multiclass anomaly detection approach on drone flight log data. 2) Propose a data-driven vector representation of the label and a severity-oriented decoding procedure of the VOLUME 12, 2024 64253 S.

The unfiltered dataset is a collection of log messages in its original condition gathered from the two sources without performing unique filtering. Therefore, there are several duplicate log messages both in training and testing sets. This is aligned with the real case scenario, where a drone tends to generate the same log message when the event is still happening. It indicates that the current event has yet to be changed or solved (if it is an issue). On the other hand, the filtered dataset is a collection of log messages after a unique filtering process. question: PREVIOUS WORKS Among the relevant published studies in the log-based anomaly detection space, sequence-based is one of the most commonly used approaches. Therefore, there are limited relevant references as this study employs a point-based approach. Below are the relevant baselines from previous research: 1) Pylogsentiment is the first study which employs sentiment analysis-based anomaly detection on operat- ing system logs using GRU and GloVe embedding. 2) SentiLog , similar to , use a two-layered BiLSTM and GloVe embedding model to perform anomaly detection on parallel file system logs. 3) TransSentLog is a further development of and uses a two-layered transformer encoder which used two attention heads and GloVe embedding along with integrated gradients to add explainability to the trained model.
answer: As noted in recent research, while most studies in log-based anomaly detection utilize a sequence-based approach, the proposed method employs a point-based approach tailored to the specific characteristics of the dataset (Silahi et al., 2025).

CONCLUSION AND FUTURE WORK In this paper, we demonstrate how to train a log-based anomaly detection model that can prioritise the prediction of higher severity anomalies on drone flight logs while increasing the prediction confidence score at the same time. Considering the nature of the dataset, where samples that belong to different severity levels share common features, a multitask vector label representation along with severity-oriented decoding is proposed. An extensive experiment proved that the proposed approach is better than the previous work baselines while being inferior to our baseline scenario based on the accuracy and F1

ground_truth: company goals by proposing a novel recommendation system for internal business processes, grounded in the COBIT 2019 framework. The system employs graph algorithms to map relationships, semantic similarity measure- ments to align enterprise goals with management objectives, and LSTM combined with TF-IDF methods to analyze and recommend specific activities. Experimental results show that this system optimizes IT management practices, enhances operational efficiency, and ensures regulatory compliance. Additionally, a user-friendly graphical representation of internal business pro- cesses using Neo4j enables stakeholders to visualize complex relationships within the COBIT framework, facilitating informed decision-making. This research advances IT governance practices by providing organizations with a valuable tool to automate and improve IT strategies, effectively achieving business objectives. Index Terms—COBIT 2019, graph algorithms, IT

question: management are vital for businesses in rapidly evolving landscape nowadays. Imple- menting an IT governance framework is crucial for several reasons. Firstly, it enables effective risk management against cyber threats, data breaches, and regulatory requirements, enhancing resilience and protecting sensitive data. Secondly, it ensures alignment between IT strategies and business ob- jectives, driving efficiency, innovation, and competitiveness. Lastly, it facilitates compliance with data privacy regulations and industry standards, ensuring transparency and accountabil- ity. Among these frameworks, COBIT is globally recognized for overseeing Enterprise Information and Technology (I&T) comprehensively, covering all systems regardless of their lo- cation within the organization. In essence, Enterprise I&T extends beyond the confines of the IT department and encompasses all facets of technology and information processing

The system employs graph algorithms to map relationships, semantic similarity measure- ments to align enterprise goals with management objectives, and LSTM combined with TF-IDF methods to analyze and recommend specific activities. Experimental results show that this system optimizes IT management practices, enhances operational efficiency, and ensures regulatory compliance. Additionally, a user-friendly graphical representation of internal business pro- cesses using Neo4j enables stakeholders to visualize complex relationships within the COBIT framework, facilitating informed decision-making. This research advances IT governance practices by providing organizations with a valuable tool to automate and improve IT strategies, effectively achieving business objectives. ground_truth: company goals by proposing a novel recommendation system for internal business processes, grounded in the COBIT 2019 framework. The system employs graph algorithms to map relationships, semantic similarity measure- ments to align enterprise goals with management objectives, and LSTM combined with TF-IDF methods to analyze and recommend specific activities. Experimental results show that this system optimizes IT management practices, enhances operational efficiency, and ensures regulatory compliance. Additionally, a user-friendly graphical representation of internal business pro- cesses using Neo4j enables stakeholders to visualize complex relationships within the COBIT framework, facilitating informed decision-making. This research advances IT governance practices by providing organizations with a valuable tool to automate and improve IT strategies, effectively achieving business objectives. question: Recall or sensitivity is the number of true positive predictions compared to all true positive data. Precision is the number of positive results compared to all positive predicted data. F-Score is the average precision and recall compared to weighting. Speciﬁcity is the number of true negative predictions compared to all negative data. Equations 15 to 19 are used to calculate these metrics. In addition to these metrics, the measurement of model performance evaluation also uses Receiver Operating Characteristics (ROC) and Area Under Curve (AUC). ABSTRACT Unlike the phased array (PA) radar that achieves array gain in proportion to the number of elements, the MIMO radar provides improvement to parameter estimation, target detection, and so on. Recently, the combination of the PA and MIMO concepts into the Phased MIMO (PMIMO) radar was proposed with overlapping non-coherent subarrays at the transmitter and an array with non-coherent elements at the receiver that proved to outperform the more conventional radars. question: a speciﬁed time delay difference of (cid:49)t, each (cid:49)t value produces a beam pattern equivalent to a phased array having inter-element phase difference that depends on the corresponding subcarrier frequencies. Multiple beams can be formed simultaneously, by assigning multiple subcarrier sets which are transmit- ted using different value of (cid:49)t. This idea is the primary contribution of this paper, which will be elaborated in the sequel. A second contribution in this paper concerns the improvement of time on target in a MIMO radar adopting CC-OFDM waveforms. While multiple beamscanning in tra- ditional phased array radar results in limited time on target, CC-OFDM MIMO radar could give a much longer time on target because multiple beams in CC-OFDM radar can be
answer: The CC-OFDM MIMO radar can achieve a longer time on target due to its ability to form multiple beams simultaneously, as demonstrated in the FPMIMO structure, which utilizes overlapping subarrays to enhance detection performance and maximize the number of detectable targets (Tahcfulloh & Hendrantoro, 2020).

question: In our case, quasi-continuous transmission is used through proper scheduling of OFDM transmission and echoes reception to prevent the coupling. • The receiver that continuously receives signal echoes coming from multiple targets must be able to distinguish which targets are very near or very far from the radar. This is the reason in this paper that orthogonal codes are given to successive CC-OFDM symbols. These unique codes are given and known by the receiver, so that the receiver could properly detect and distinguish targets nearby and far away from the radar. ABSTRACT Unlike the phased array (PA) radar that achieves array gain in proportion to the number of elements, the MIMO radar provides improvement to parameter estimation, target detection, and so on. Recently, the combination of the PA and MIMO concepts into the Phased MIMO (PMIMO) radar was proposed with overlapping non-coherent subarrays at the transmitter and an array with non-coherent elements at the receiver that proved to outperform the more conventional radars. ABSTRACT Unlike the phased array (PA) radar that achieves array gain in proportion to the number of elements, the MIMO radar provides improvement to parameter estimation, target detection, and so on. Recently, the combination of the PA and MIMO concepts into the Phased MIMO (PMIMO) radar was proposed with overlapping non-coherent subarrays at the transmitter and an array with non-coherent elements at the receiver that proved to outperform the more conventional radars. Using the same procedure, the 63 beam signals are generated simultaneously by the digital WG at each transmit element. For a good operational performance of CC OFDM MIMO radar an important and crucial note should be made now, that is: Transmitting multiple symbols in series and in parallel contributing simultaneously to multiple beams effective in various range intervals, the quasi - CW transmission neces- sitates that the CC OFDM symbols should be orthogonal in time, frequency and code. In the following the orthogonality aspects are detailed. • Single CC OFDM symbol In this example, one symbol of CC-OFDM waveform contributes to 63 beam signals.
answer: As noted in the study by Kuswidiastuti et al. (2025), the orthogonality of the symbol series in CC-OFDM MIMO radar allows for effective separation of received signals, thereby enhancing operational performance in various range intervals.

THE COUPLING BETWEEN NEIGHBORING BEAMS This section analyzes the coupling between beams which are transmitted simultaneously over the same period of sym- bol duration. Analysis is done to the beams transmitted on the ﬁrst symbol period, with the scheduling as described in Table 2. It is necessary to make sure that the coupling between the multi-beam CC-OFDM which are transmitted simultaneously using limited bandwidth are low to avoid the probability of false alarm on radar receiver. ground_truth: together with transmit scheduling to avoid transmit receive coupling. The receive scheduling is also related to the time scheduling of the correlation between symbols, which is an important stage in the beam and range processing. The transmit and receive time schedule of CC-OFDM radar is described in Fig. 4. The receive scheduling is very important, especially in a multiple target scheme. The echo signals caused by two or more targets at different ranges (i.e., one target is near to the radar, and the other is far) could cause range ambiguity. The clock synchronization between the multiple transmitters and the multiple receivers of CC-OFDM radars are very crucial to guarantee that the receiver can give the accurate detection. It can be

question: beam isolation, the range resolution, the Doppler resolution, those parameters can be modiﬁed and optimized based on the characteristic of the target of interest. Some of the radar speciﬁcations that can be achieved by modifying some of the CC-OFDM waveform parameters are as follows: • The maximum unambiguous range for conventional OFDM radar is mainly limited by the spacing between the OFDM subcarriers. However, one could extend the maximum unambiguous range of the radar by send- ing multiple successive orthogonal CC-OFDM symbols containing orthogonal Golay codes. How many symbols that must be sent will depend on how far the maximum unambiguous range is that the application requires. • The maximum velocity depends on the frequency spac- ing between the OFDM
answer: As discussed in the context of CC-OFDM MIMO radar, the maximum unambiguous range can be extended by optimizing waveform parameters and utilizing multiple orthogonal symbols (Kuswidiastuti et al., 2025).

ABSTRACT Unlike the phased array (PA) radar that achieves array gain in proportion to the number of elements, the MIMO radar provides improvement to parameter estimation, target detection, and so on. Recently, the combination of the PA and MIMO concepts into the Phased MIMO (PMIMO) radar was proposed with overlapping non-coherent subarrays at the transmitter and an array with non-coherent elements at the receiver that proved to outperform the more conventional radars. ground_truth: and radial velocity detection of targets is not discussed yet. The contribution of this paper is in the receiver processing of CC-OFDM-MIMO radar, which is different from those in other types of OFDM MIMO radars or other multi-TRM radars due to the nature of the orthogonal multi-beam generation with Golay-coded OFDM waveforms and subbands. There are previous research on radar waveform for ISAC purposes , which discussed about cyclic prefix (CP) based OFDM waveform and how the CP is very much defining the maximum unambiguous range of the radar system. CC-OFDM MIMO radar waveform design which is discussed in this paper is not a CP based radar, the transmitters are transmitting multiple orthogonal symbols in series without any guard time

ground_truth: Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) , is a variation of Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN) that has been successfully applied to various Natural Language Processing (NLP) tasks. LSTM has the ability to capture long-range dependencies with sequences making it suitable for solving sequence labeling problems , , , such as Named Entity Recognition tagging (POS-Tag) and Neural (NER), Part-of-Speech combining (NMT). Study Machine Translation Bidirectional LSTM (BiLSTM) model with Conditional Random Field (CRF) to solve NER and POS-Tag Task. BiLSTM encodes word or character vectors into features, while CRF is a classifier to predict the target labels. Previous research has explored enhancing the feature extractor by integrating serves attention mechanisms with existing models or introducing alternative extractors such as Convolutional Neural Networks

question: I. INTRODUCTION I. INTRODUCTION The use of drones has become increasingly common in various applications, including aerial photography, surveying, and monitoring. However, drones can also be involved in incidents such as accidents, violations, or even crimes, making it necessary to investigate drone logs as part of forensic investigations. In a forensic investigation, drone logs are essential sources of information that can help investigators reconstruct the events leading up to an incident. Drone logs typically include information such as flight paths, altitude, speed, battery life, sensor data, and other telemetry information . Important analysis of drone logs involves the identification of the most critical pieces of information in the log data and the analysis of how they relate to the incident
answer: As noted by Silahie et al. (2023), drone logs are essential sources of information in forensic investigations, aiding in the reconstruction of incident scenarios and the identification of critical data points.

CONCLUSION AND FUTURE WORKS In this research, we have experimented with the employ- ment of cosine similarity as a substitute for dot-product self-attention in the encoder sub-layer of Transformer archi- tecture. To evaluate our proposed approach, we construct our own NER dataset by manually extracting several drone forensic image datasets that are publicly available from the VTO Labs. ground_truth: Transformer-Based NER on Drone Flight Logs to Support Forensic Investigation Accordingly, Jain et al. proposed a framework com- prising 12 phases. The ﬁrst ﬁve phases were used to locate and validate the drone’s sensors and data. The other seven steps were used to analyze physical evidence like ﬁngerprints and digital evidence from several sources, such as memory cards, ﬂight logs, and network logs. However, the proposed framework has not explained the preparation phase in detail. Therefore, another framework consisting of four investigative phases with a more comprehensive analysis is proposed by Al-Dhaqm et al. . One of the primary distinctions of this framework is a more extensive preparation phase consisting of pre- and post-incident preparation. Pre-incident prepara- tion is an

question: presented a novel multi-head attention mechanism that uses various atten- tion functions to capture diverse contextual elements. The multi-head attention approach allows attention to be simul- taneously allocated to information from different subspaces and representational positions. In contrast to soft attention, which focuses attention softly on the entire input to produce a context representation with weights determined based on the relevance of each part of the input, soft attention relies on only one attention mechanism. Kumar et al. developed the multi-head attention- based BiLSTM (MHA-BiLSTM) model. The results are quite promising compared to the use of single-head attention. How- ever, using the BiLSTM model is computationally expensive, especially when dealing with large datasets . BiLSTM is also prone to overfitting,

ground_truth: Based on the results presented in Table 3, the multi-head attention module positively impacts the model’s performance. When multi-head attention in our proposed model is replaced with self-attention, the testing accuracy drops from 79% to 71%. Furthermore, if we remove the attention component in the aggregation layer and only use soft alignment attention in the matching layer, the testing accuracy is 72%. In the last experiment, we used only BiLSTM for inference composition, which resulted in a test accuracy of 71%. These results provide evidence that the absence of the multi-head attention module can decrease the model’s performance. While multi-head attention is effective in capturing long-range dependencies and global contextual information, it tends to be less adept at modeling sequential

The choice between one-stage and two-stage object detec- tion models depends heavily on the specific demands of the task, particularly in medical imaging contexts such as colorectal cancer (CRC), squamous cell carcinoma (SCC), gastric lesions, and polypoid lesions. One-stage detectors, such as YOLO and SSD, excel in speed and computational efficiency, making them ideal for real-time applications. For instance, YOLOv5 demonstrated remarkable performance in CRC detection with nearly perfect metrics—precision (99.071%), recall (98.064%), and mAP (98.8%)—allowing clinicians to analyze colonoscopy videos efficiently during live procedures . These errors are likely due to the imbalanced dataset, which predominantly consists of normal or frequently occurring abnormalities, limiting the model's ability to generalize to less common cases. To evaluate the model’s ability to detect rare abnormalities, we analyzed performance on a subset of low- frequency labels as shown in Table V. Results indicate that while overall precision remains high, recall for these classes is lower (e.g., 0.62 for Cardiomegaly vs. 0.81 average), confirming the effect of class imbalance. Incorporating targeted sampling or multi-label loss re-weighting could help address this in future work. The proposed research is based on a simplified deep learn- ing architecture and channel selection approach that leads towards closer and resource-constrained real-time implemen- tation. Cross-subject validation has a better generaliza- tion model than dependent-subject validation. question: DATASET The DEAP dataset has 32 participants with a composition of 50% male and 50% female samples . The stimulus used is a video with a duration of 60 seconds. The results of the EEG recording have a duration of 63 seconds, of which 3 seconds are the baseline. One participant has 40 recordings of the EEG trials. The results of the EEG trial recordings have been mea- sured using the Self-Assessment Manikin (SAM). SAM has four emotional dimensions, namely valence, arousal, domi- nance, and liking, with ratings 0-9. For valence and arousal classes, emotion labels are divided into two categories: low and high. The LSTM model was chosen for its ability to handle short-term and long-term data through gating, mitigate the vanishing gradient problem, and efficiently manage variable-length input sequences [22]. This model is particularly well-suited for time series data where long-term context is important. ground_truth: I. INTRODUCTION I. INTRODUCTION The use of drones has become increasingly common in various applications, including aerial photography, surveying, and monitoring. However, drones can also be involved in incidents such as accidents, violations, or even crimes, making it necessary to investigate drone logs as part of forensic investigations. In a forensic investigation, drone logs are essential sources of information that can help investigators reconstruct the events leading up to an incident. Drone logs typically include information such as flight paths, altitude, speed, battery life, sensor data, and other telemetry information . Important analysis of drone logs involves the identification of the most critical pieces of information in the log data and the analysis of how they relate to the incident

As drone forensics is an emerging topic, there are very limited public datasets available. The log messages used in this study are mainly acquired from DJI-made devices. There is yet to come a publicly available dataset of log messages from other drone manufacturers. Several publicly available datasets are mainly about sensor data and multimedia artefacts . question: LLMs to detect anomalous log entries in a point-based approach . However, existing studies only detect whether an event or subsequent event is an anomaly. In a forensic setting, analysing a large number of log entries resulting from binary anomaly detection is considered inefficient and labour-intensive as the detection is performed on the acquired evidence at one time . Therefore, too many detected anomalies need to be detailed into a more fine- grained detection to help the investigator better prioritise the analysis objective. As a result, this study extends the binary anomaly detection to multi-class anomaly detection, as exemplified by another domain application such as plane flight data , image data , and internet of vehicle . Generally, log-based anomaly detection can be performed using either point, collective, or contextual approaches. In the log-based anomaly detection literature, most of the existing studies propose either a contextual or collective approach, where the decision is taken by examining a sequence of log events. However, detecting abnormalities in log data can be performed using a point-based approach, where one single log record is the only feature needed. question: Existing anomaly detection modelling techniques can be conducted either in a point, contextual, or collective setting. Point anomaly detection characterises each log entry, whether an event is an abnormality or a normal event. For instance, if a user types the wrong password when trying to gain access to an operating system prompt, the generated log message can be considered as an anomaly , , . As for contextual anomaly, the detection involves analysing some contextually related attributes to determine whether a log entry is an anomaly. question: a log entry is an anomaly. As an example, the flight path taken by a drone during a flight is recorded in longitude and latitude coordinates. These data can be used to detect an anomaly by finding a peculiar value within the log records by contrasting the normal values to abnormal values using a certain threshold . Unique to the previous two approaches, collective anomaly establishes anomaly criteria based on a set of instances associated with each other, distinct from the overall data. Statistical anomaly detection is one of the common cases for this type of anomaly detection. Another case scenario in log-based anomaly detection is a sequence-based approach, where an abnormality is determined by analysing a group of events

Generally, log-based anomaly detection can be performed using either point, collective, or contextual approaches. In the log-based anomaly detection literature, most of the existing studies propose either a contextual or collective approach, where the decision is taken by examining a sequence of log events. However, detecting abnormalities in log data can be performed using a point-based approach, where one single log record is the only feature needed. Therefore, several studies explored utilising a point-based approach to detect anomalous events on logs data, such as in operating systems , , drone devices , and distribution systems , . CONCLUSION AND FUTURE WORK In this paper, we demonstrate how to train a log-based anomaly detection model that can prioritise the prediction of higher severity anomalies on drone flight logs while increasing the prediction confidence score at the same time. Considering the nature of the dataset, where samples that belong to different severity levels share common features, a multitask vector label representation along with severity-oriented decoding is proposed. An extensive experiment proved that the proposed approach is better than the previous work baselines while being inferior to our baseline scenario based on the accuracy and F1

question: align with the anomaly severity context, where the distance between true and predicted labels matters. If the predicted label is lower than the true label, we define it False Positive (FP). Conversely, if the true label is lower than the predicted label, we define it False Negative (FN). In this context, having less FP is more important than having less FN, as the task objective is anomaly severity detection, where we aim to detect all abnormalities along with the severity level. Therefore, undetected abnormalities, or detecting higher severity as lower ones, indicate that the model fails to meet the task’s objectives. Fig 14 shows the comparison of the confusion matrix between experimented scenarios and a baseline from previous work .

question: on critical findings. The proposed embedding, DroneSBERT, improved the trustworthiness of the model, as evidenced by the word’s importance report. Despite the promising results, the model’s generality remains untested as the training dataset contains only log messages acquired from 82 drone models, mostly from DJI. This is one of the main challenges in drone forensics research, where human-readable flight log datasets from other drone models are unavailable. In the future, we plan to explore the flight logs of other drone models to train more general anomaly detection models. As for the method, multi- objective learning that employs multiple loss functions to train an anomaly detection model will be explored. In this section, challenges encountered during the study are disclosed, along with limitations and threats to validity. As drone forensics is an emerging topic, there are very limited public datasets available. The log messages used in this study are mainly acquired from DJI-made devices. There is yet to come a publicly available dataset of log messages from other drone manufacturers. Several publicly available datasets are mainly about sensor data and multimedia artefacts . While in this study, we solely depend on the human-readable messages generated by the drone during a flight. ground_truth: DATASET The DEAP dataset has 32 participants with a composition of 50% male and 50% female samples . The stimulus used is a video with a duration of 60 seconds. The results of the EEG recording have a duration of 63 seconds, of which 3 seconds are the baseline. One participant has 40 recordings of the EEG trials. The results of the EEG trial recordings have been mea- sured using the Self-Assessment Manikin (SAM). SAM has four emotional dimensions, namely valence, arousal, domi- nance, and liking, with ratings 0-9. For valence and arousal classes, emotion labels are divided into two categories: low and high. ground_truth: DATASET The DEAP dataset has 32 participants with a composition of 50% male and 50% female samples . The stimulus used is a video with a duration of 60 seconds. The results of the EEG recording have a duration of 63 seconds, of which 3 seconds are the baseline. One participant has 40 recordings of the EEG trials. The results of the EEG trial recordings have been mea- sured using the Self-Assessment Manikin (SAM). SAM has four emotional dimensions, namely valence, arousal, domi- nance, and liking, with ratings 0-9. For valence and arousal classes, emotion labels are divided into two categories: low and high. Models pretrained on these datasets have optimized weights for recognizing common features, mitigating issues associated with small datasets. Transfer learning and fine-tuning allow models to adapt to new datasets, significantly reducing training time. Studies utilizing pretrained models include for detect- ing polyps with YOLOv4, for Crohn’s disease lesions, for pre-colorectal cancer abnormalities, for bladder cancer lesions, and for cervical lesions. question: can be fine-tuned into our dataset. V. CONCLUSION V. CONCLUSION This research demonstrates that the YOLOv8 model, specifically the YOLOv8m variant, provides excellent results in detecting Peritoneal Carcinomatosis (PC) from the used laparoscopic image dataset. By utilizing and fine-tuning the pre-trained YOLOv8 weights, this model shows superior performance with a mean Average Precision (mAP50) of 0.871, precision of 0.893, recall of 0.785, and an F1 score of 0.84. The predicted images also show that the performance of YOLOv8 with pre-trained weights is able to predict images well. All YOLOv8 scales are accurate in making predictions, with no errors in image prediction. In contrast, without using pre-trained weights, YOLOv8n and YOLOv8s still make errors in prediction, with some lesions being missed.
answer: This research demonstrates that the YOLOv8 model, specifically the YOLOv8m variant, provides excellent results in detecting Peritoneal Carcinomatosis (PC) from the used laparoscopic image dataset (Rochmawati et al., 2025).

Models pretrained on these datasets have optimized weights for recognizing common features, mitigating issues associated with small datasets. Transfer learning and fine-tuning allow models to adapt to new datasets, significantly reducing training time. Studies utilizing pretrained models include for detect- ing polyps with YOLOv4, for Crohn’s disease lesions, for pre-colorectal cancer abnormalities, for bladder cancer lesions, and for cervical lesions.
